_doc: Raven Hollow NPC life layer (BACKLOG #88). Consumed by NPCLifeSystem (scripts/systems/npc_life_system.gd). Purely additive ambient life: per-role/per-region/per-zone bark pools spoken as floating speech bubbles, NPC-to-NPC chatter exchanges between friends, seeded friendship/rivalry role-pairs, daily job routes (home-relative waypoint offsets) and an inn-rest window at night. Registered NPCs are re-homed toward route waypoints / the inn by the DayNight clock; unregistered NPCs and a headless boot with no world are untouched. Roles match npc ids/roles from scripts/npc_data.gd + data/npc_schedules.json; region/zone keys match scripts/zone_defs.gd.

merchant: Best prices this side of the river.
innkeeper: Mind the ravens on the road.
Room for one more by the fire.
baker: Bread before the frost sets in.
herbalist: Mind what you pick out there.
The moor gives, if you know it.
gravekeeper: The dead keep their own counsel.
Another for the hill by dusk.
priest: Light a candle for the lost.
The stone hears, so speak soft.
guard: Trouble on the east road, they say.
maid: Dust settles the moment I turn.
hunter: Tracked something big past the tree line.
Keep your blade close out there.
wanderer: Follow the ravens, they eat well.
child: Ma says stay off the wall!
Did you see the big dog?
The old stone talks at night!
drunk: The... the ravens moved it, I swear.
Best ale in... in the whole... place.

border: Stay clear of the live stones.
The rot's arriving in real time.
west: Full granary, empty bellies. Queer times.
Angel Wings takes the grain, leaves the mud.
Pray to the Queen's saints, if you must.
north: Wind off the steppe cuts to the bone.
Gravemark's no place for the living.
east: Ridge passes are choked with snow.
Keep your lantern lit up there.
south: Ash on the wind from the vents.
The gift country asks a price.
coast: Fog rolls thick off the piers.
Salt in everything, even the bread.
The canal barges run at dawn.

iron_vein: The river runs the color of old blood.
Something's been digging near the dolmen.
vetka: Old Marta's herbs keep the fever off.
The first monster's just a symptom.
copper_wells: The handprints came back on the wall.

generic: The road's long, rest a while.

lines: Colder than last week, aye?
Frost'll take the last of the barley.
Best get the beasts in early, then.
Aye. Long winter coming.
More ravens on the hill today.
They gather where the dead do.
Ill omen, that.
Or just hungry. Same thing, lately.
Prices are up at the market.
Merchant's a thief in a good coat.
Ha! Aren't they all.
Still, coin's coin. Buy me an ale?
Heard trouble on the east road.
Bandits, or worse?
Worse, they say. Best not travel alone.
Then I'll walk with you a while.
The smith's sweet on the innkeeper, you know.
Everyone knows but the smith.
Ha! Poor fool.
Say nothing. Let him find his own way.
The old stone hummed again at dusk.
You heard it too?
The gravekeeper says don't listen.
Wise man. I'll keep to my hearth.
Fields are near cleared.
A good year, for once.
Don't tempt fate saying so.
Fair. I'll knock on wood.
